In terms of the hybrid portion, guess what? Well, two things. Number one, I want you to try to develop sales aids. There's any time you can, if you're out in the field, you're going to have some sort of brochure or questionnaire form or something that makes sense that you could be carrying with you as part of your presentation. Like for instance, when a doctor does an intake form, he's got his chart there. He's asking questions. So you wouldn't be like, so how long you're living in the air? That's going to be stupid. When I was in the mortgage industry, as I said, right, I would say, oh, you're paying more than this. Wow, I could definitely save you money. I could put how long you live in the area. Uh-huh. And I would be like, so how long you're living in the area? It was if I knew my questions, I just had that there for their benefit. Not like I needed them to ask the questions. That's the case. So body language, confidence. But by doing that, you have the confidence because they're in front of you. They're your guards.
